3.happening: The left boundary starts at index 0.
3.why: The search window begins as the entire array.
3.invariant: The target, if present, lies between left and right.

4.happening: The right boundary starts at the last index.
4.why: The window must cover every element at the start.
4.invariant: left and right enclose all candidate positions.

5.happening: We continue while left has not passed right.
5.why: An empty window means the target is not present.
5.invariant: Each iteration considers a non-empty window.

6.happening: We compute mid as the middle of the window.
6.why: Comparing the middle element halves the search each step.
6.invariant: mid always lies between left and right.

7.happening: nums[{mid}] equals target, so we have found it.
7.why: A direct hit at the midpoint ends the search immediately.
7.invariant: The returned index holds the target value.

7.why: We must decide which half could still contain target.
7.invariant: The target, if present, is on one side of mid.

8.happening: We return mid, the index of the target.
8.why: The value at mid matches the target.

9.happening: We test whether the left half is sorted.
9.why: Exactly one half of a rotated array is always in order, and a sorted half is easy to reason about.
9.invariant: If nums[left] <= nums[mid], the values from left to mid are ascending.

10.happening: We check whether target falls within the sorted left half.
10.why: If target is inside the ordered range, the answer must be there.
10.invariant: A target between nums[left] and nums[mid] can only be in the left half.

11.happening: We move right to mid - 1, keeping only the left half.
11.why: The target lies in the sorted left half, so the right half is discarded.
11.invariant: The window still contains the target if it is present.

13.happening: We move left to mid + 1, keeping only the right half.
13.why: The target is not in the sorted left half, so it must be to the right.
13.invariant: Discarding the left half never drops the target.

15.happening: The right half is sorted, so we check whether target falls within it.
15.why: A target inside the ordered right range must be on that side.
15.invariant: A target between nums[mid] and nums[right] can only be in the right half.

16.happening: We move left to mid + 1, keeping only the right half.
16.why: The target lies in the sorted right half, so the left half is discarded.
16.invariant: The window still contains the target if it is present.

18.happening: We move right to mid - 1, keeping only the left half.
18.why: The target is not in the sorted right half, so it must be to the left.
18.invariant: Discarding the right half never drops the target.

19.happening: The window is empty, so we return -1.
19.why: Every position has been ruled out without a match.
19.invariant: target does not appear in nums.

init.happening: Set the search window to the whole array.
init.why: Binary search begins by considering every element.
init.invariant: left and right enclose all candidate positions.

loop.happening: Check that the window still holds elements.
loop.why: The search runs until it finds the target or empties the window.
loop.invariant: Each pass halves the remaining window.

update.happening: Compute the midpoint of the window.
update.why: The midpoint is the pivot for every comparison.
update.invariant: mid is always inside the current window.

check.happening: Compare the midpoint and decide which half is sorted and where target could be.
check.why: In a rotated array, identifying the sorted half tells us which side to keep.
check.invariant: Only a half that could contain the target is ever kept.

move.happening: Collapse the window to the surviving half.
move.why: Discarding half the elements each step gives logarithmic time.
move.invariant: The retained window still contains the target if it is present.

return.happening: Report the result — the index found or -1.
return.why: The search has either matched the target or exhausted the window.
return.invariant: The returned value correctly reflects the target's presence.
